column1: # Belongs here: the 〜そう appearance form built from an i- or na-adjective stem
# (高そう, 便利そう). They all inflect as na-adjectives, so type stays
# na-adjective even though this is a grammar pattern, not core vocabulary.

column4: looking happy (〜そう, from うれしい)
seems fun; looks enjoyable (〜そう, from 楽しい)
looks high/tall/expensive (〜そう, from 高い)
looks cheap (〜そう, from 安い)
looks new (〜そう, from 新しい)
looks old (〜そう, from 古い)
looks convenient (〜そう, from 便利)
looks famous (〜そう, from 有名)
looks quiet (〜そう, from 静か)
looks serious; tough (〜そう, from 大変)
